title: Holding a Gold Short ($XAUUSD): Why Technical Patience Beats FOMO
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hi everyone, as a developer obsessed with process optimization, I wanted to share this ongoing trade on Gold. We are currently sitting on a setup with a **3.43 Risk/Reward (RR) ratio**, and it’s a textbook case of why market structure matters. Here is the link that allowed me to do that : [https://hercul-market.vercel.app](https://hercul-market.vercel.app)

**Technical Trade Details (M5):**

* **The Signal:** Identification of a clear bearish market structure break after a failed bullish continuation on the highs.
* **The Entry:** Position taken on the retest of the Order Block identified by my dashboard's AI.
* **The Target:** Aiming for an untapped liquidity zone lower down, with a tight Stop Loss above the last pivot.

**Why I use Hercul Market (Next.js/Supabase) to manage this:**

1. **Zero Emotion:** When the price stalls, the AI reminds me that the structure remains bearish. This prevents cutting the trade too early out of fear of seeing gains evaporate.
2. **Real-Time Dashboarding:** No more manual **XIRR** calculations on a buggy Excel sheet. The trade is logged in a PostgreSQL database, and my net performance updates instantly.
3. **Technical Discipline:** The tool visually displays if the RR ratio is worth the risk. At 3.43, the mathematical probabilities are heavily on my side over the long run.

Trading isn't about "guessing" the next candle; it’s about data management and probabilities. If you want to test the structure analyzer on your own charts, the beta is open at the link above.

**Would you have held the position or taken profits at the first intermediate support?** 📉📈
